source.title: Dissociating Frontal Lobe Lesion Induced Deficits in Rule Value Learning Using Reinforcement Learning Models and a WCST Analog.

confidence_rationale: Strong macaque lesion-behaviour study with computational-model decomposition; recent (2025); directly dissociates rule-value updating (OFC), rule maintenance (PS), and mode-switching from negative feedback (ACC). Substantial support for distributed multi-region rule architecture (ARC-063 strong reading) and against single-site dominance.
failure_signatures: If ARC-062 weak reading concentrates rule-context modulation at a single site (PFC-side score_bias OR BG-side score-aggregation OR hippocampal-side trajectory-proposal), Capkova et al's evidence predicts that single-site instantiation will fail under task-condition perturbation that taxes a different sub-function (e.g. negative-feedback handling, working-memory rule-maintenance) — points the cluster toward distributed multi-site instantiation as Phase 4 / GAP-E becomes load-bearing.

If ARC-062 PASSes Phase 2 falsifier but fails on a learning-rate-asymmetry probe (positive-feedback vs negative-feedback rule updating), the lesion data predicts the missing functionality maps to ACC analog — V3 substrate has dACC (SD-032b) but the rule-context coupling is not yet wired.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Distinct frontal regions make dissociable contributions to rule-guided decision-making. Macaques with lesions to PS (principal sulcus), ACC (anterior cingulate), OFC (orbitofrontal), sdlPFC (superior dorsolateral PFC), or FPC (frontopolar) performed a WCST analog. RL model parameter recovery showed: OFC lesions decreased rule value updating rate from BOTH positive and negative feedback; PS lesions left rule value updating intact but impaired choice repetition under well-established rule values (working-memory maintenance deficit); ACC lesions produced specific deficit in learning from negative feedback and impaired the switch between trial-and-error and working-memory modes in response to increased error likelihood.
mapping.ree_translation: Strong direct support for distributed multi-region rule architecture, which favours ARC-063 strong reading over ARC-062 weak reading at the architectural-end-state level. The WCST analog dissociation maps three distinct sub-functions to three distinct regions: rule-value updating (OFC analog), rule-maintenance under stable rules (PS / lateral PFC analog — REE's SD-033a), and mode-switching driven by negative feedback (ACC analog — REE's dACC SD-032b + closure SD-034). For ARC-062 weak-reading Phase 1 / Phase 2 falsifier, the implication is that the gated-policy + discriminator architecture is operating on the rule-maintenance leg (PS analog) only — the rule-value-updating and negative-feedback-switching legs are NOT wired into the discriminator's training signal. This is consistent with ARC-062 being a *necessary but insufficient* substrate; PASS on SD-054 is plausible but performance ceiling depends on whether the OFC and ACC analog functions become rate-limiting.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The WCST analog tests abstract dimensional rules (e.g. shape vs colour matching). The reef vs forage discriminative cut on SD-054 is a context rule, not a dimensional rule. The mapping from PS-rule-maintenance to SD-033a's rule_state is direct; the mapping from OFC-rule-value-updating to a REE substrate is less clean (the OFC analog substrate landed 2026-04-26, but the rule-value-updating-rate machinery is not yet wired). Some transfer risk on the negative-feedback-mode-switching mapping to dACC + SD-034 closure since the WCST mode-switching is to-and-from a working-memory state, not closure of a goal.
mapping.source_context: Macaque frontal lesion + WCST analog behavioural data + three-parameter RL model parameter recovery; recent (2025), peer-reviewed.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality — recent peer-reviewed lesion study with quantitative model-based dissociation. Mapping fidelity strong: three sub-functions map to three REE substrates already in implementation. Transfer risk moderate: WCST dimensional rules differ from SD-054 context rules, and the rule-value-updating substrate is the weakest cross-mapping.
